title.S: Stuffed Shells with Spinach and Ricotta

description.S: Jumbo pasta shells stuffed with a creamy, cheesy spinach filling, topped with rich marinara sauce and mozzarella cheese, baked to bubbly perfection. This delicious dinner can be made in advance and it's freezer friendly too!

L.S: 24-26 Jumbo Pasta Shells (cook extra, some will tear)
2 tablespoons Olive Oil, divided
Kosher Salt, for pasta water
3 cloves Garlic, minced
4 cups packed Fresh Spinach Leaves, roughly chopped (about 5 oz)
15 ounces whole-milk Ricotta Cheese
1 large Egg, lightly beaten
3 cups Organic Shredded Mozzarella Cheese, divided
1/2 cup Freshly Grated Parmesan Cheese, plus extra for serving
1 teaspoon Italian Seasoning
1/4 teaspoon Crushed Red Pepper Flakes
1/2 teaspoon Kosher Salt
1/4 teaspoon Freshly Ground Black Pepper
3 cups Marinara Sauce, divided (homemade or store-bought)
2 tablespoons Fresh Basil Leaves, roughly chopped, for garnish

note.S: Freshly grated, plus extra for serving

L.S: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F. Lightly spray a 9x13-inch baking dish with nonstick cooking spray and set aside.

Bring a large pot of generously salted water to a rolling boil. Cook 24-26 jumbo pasta shells until just shy of al dente, about 1 minute less than package directions. Drain carefully, rinse with cold water to stop cooking and prevent sticking, then spread on a sheet pan and drizzle with 1 tablespoon olive oil, tossing gently. Set aside.

Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the 3 cloves of minced garlic and saute for 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to brown. Add the 4 cups of chopped spinach and cook, stirring frequently, for 1-2 minutes until fully wilted. Transfer the spinach to a fine-mesh strainer or clean kitchen towel and squeeze out as much excess moisture as possible. Let cool briefly.

Combine the 15 ounces of ricotta cheese, 1 lightly beaten egg, 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese, 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese, 1 teaspoon Italian seasoning, 1/4 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes, 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, and 1/4 teaspoon black pepper in a large bowl. Stir until well combined. Fold in the squeezed, cooled spinach mixture. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.

Spread 1 and 1/2 cups of marinara sauce evenly across the bottom of the prepared baking dish. Fill each intact cooked shell with approximately 2-3 tablespoons of filling using a spoon or piping bag. Nestle the filled shells open-side up in the dish in a single layer. Spoon the remaining 1 and 1/2 cups of marinara sauce over and between the shells. Sprinkle the remaining 2 cups of shredded mozzarella cheese evenly over the top.
Cover the dish tightly with aluminum foil. Bake for 30 minutes until the sauce is bubbling around the edges.

Remove the foil and switch the oven to broil on high. Position the rack 6 inches from the heating element. Broil for 2-3 minutes, watching constantly, until the mozzarella is golden brown and bubbly. Do not walk away as cheese can go from golden to burnt in seconds.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the filling to set so the shells hold together when served.
Garnish with 2 tablespoons of fresh chopped basil and an extra dusting of grated Parmesan if desired. Serve warm.
Extra shells are insurance. Jumbo shells are fragile and often crack. Cook 4-6 extra so you have 20 perfect ones ready to fill.
Whole-milk ricotta makes a significant difference in creaminess. Avoid part-skim if possible.
Do not skip squeezing the spinach. Excess moisture is the number one cause of watery, disappointing stuffed shells.

Make ahead (refrigerator): Assemble the dish through the assembly step, cover tightly with foil, and refrigerate for up to 24 hours. When ready, bake straight from the fridge and add 5-10 extra minutes of covered baking time.

Freezer friendly: Assemble through the assembly step, cover with a layer of plastic wrap pressed directly against the surface, then foil. Freeze for up to 3 months. To serve, thaw overnight in the refrigerator, then let sit at room temperature for 20-30 minutes while the oven preheats. Bake covered for 35-40 minutes, then broil as directed.
Food safety: Ensure the internal temperature reaches 160 degrees F due to the raw egg in the filling before serving.
For a gluten-free version, substitute the jumbo pasta shells with a gluten-free jumbo shell pasta such as Barilla or Banza brand.
